title: Monitor collector health using Grafana Fleet Management

blocks.content:
Welcome to the Grafana learning path that teaches you how to use Grafana Fleet Management to monitor and troubleshoot issues with your collectors.

Fleet Management is a Grafana Cloud feature that helps you manage multiple telemetry collector deployments at scale. You can view fleet health, monitor individual collectors, assign configurations based on attributes, and remotely configure collectors.

## Here's what to expect

When you complete this journey, you'll be able to:

- Understand the value of Grafana Fleet Management
- Determine if a collector has been registered with Fleet Management
- Create an access token and register a collector with Fleet Management
- Interpret Fleet Management dashboards so that you can troubleshoot issues with your collectors

## Before you begin

Before you monitor collectors using Fleet Management, ensure that you have:

- A Grafana Cloud account. To create an account, refer to [Grafana Cloud](https://grafana.com/signup/cloud/connect-account).
- Grafana Cloud administrator privileges and the ability to create and edit access policies.
- Access to the host or machine where Alloy is installed.
- The ability to set environment variables on the machine where Alloy is running.
- A current Grafana Alloy version. Fleet Management must be used with Alloy collectors and is recommended for Alloy v1.7.2 or later.
- Knowledge of how to start and stop a service on the machine where Alloy is installed.

## Troubleshooting

If you get stuck, we've got your back. Where appropriate, troubleshooting information is just a click away.

## More to explore

We understand you might want to explore other capabilities not strictly on this path. We'll provide you opportunities where it makes sense.

---

### Related paths

This journey expects Grafana Alloy to be installed. If you don't yet have Grafana Alloy installed, any of these related paths will help you.

- [Monitor a Linux server in Grafana Cloud](/docs/learning-paths/linux-server-integration/)
- [Monitor Kubernetes cluster infrastructure in Grafana Cloud](/docs/learning-paths/kubernetes/)
- [Send logs to Grafana Cloud using Alloy](/docs/learning-paths/send-logs-alloy-loki/)
- [Monitor a macOS system in Grafana Cloud](/docs/learning-paths/macos-integration/)
